stories.headline: US sought 20-year nuclear moratorium from Iran; Tehran offered far less: Report
stories.summary: The US sought a 20-year moratorium on Iran's nuclear activity, but Tehran countered with a significantly shorter period, reportedly a single-digit number of years. US Vice President JD Vance led the negotiations, which ended without an agreement. The US proposed a 20-year suspension with additional restrictions, but Iran refused to commit to forgoing a nuclear weapon. The Iranian delegation's counteroffer was for a period of up to 5 years, suspending nuclear activity. The US considered its offer the "final and best" deal, but Iran declined to accept the terms. The negotiations concluded a day before the US was set to take further action, with President Trump threatening to interdict vessels and destroy mines in international waters. The talks have stalled, with no agreement reached on Iran's nuclear program. Technical discussions are expected to follow the high-level talks.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran wants deal, vows no nuclear weapons agreement
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that Iran wants to make a deal, following stalled nuclear talks. Trump said Iran has expressed interest in an agreement, but warned that the US will not accept any deal allowing Tehran to obtain nuclear weapons. He emphasized that Iran must agree to "no nuclear weapons" as a condition of a potential deal. Trump reiterated that "Iran will never have a nuclear weapon" and that if they don't agree, there will be no deal. He also stated that the US will leave Iran when they are certain the regime cannot build a nuclear weapon "for years". Trump's comments came after 14-hour negotiations concluded the first phase in Islamabad, with Trump saying "we win regardless" of the outcome. The US has maintained a firm stance on Iran's nuclear program.

stories.headline: Iran war: Talks collapse is bad news for ordinary Iranians
stories.summary: Peace negotiations between the US and Iran collapsed in Islamabad after a 21-hour marathon session. The collapse of talks is expected to negatively impact ordinary Iranians, who have been affected by nearly six weeks of heavy bombings and airstrikes. The US has responded to the failed talks by imposing a blockade on ships entering and leaving Iran's ports, starting Monday. The blockade was announced by US President Donald Trump. Meanwhile, talks between Israel and Lebanon are expected to take place in Washington next week, amid intensified attacks between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, which have resulted in at least 1,953 deaths in Lebanon. The situation remains volatile, with a shaky ceasefire in place in Iran.

stories.headline: Marcos’s overture to China masks anxiety about US reliability
stories.summary: The Philippines has stated that any energy deals with China must respect its sovereignty. This comes as China has warned the Philippines against turning the grounded boat BRP Sierra Madre into a significant military outpost in the disputed waters. Meanwhile, China continues to build its own military outposts in the area. The Philippines' overture to China is seen as a move to balance its relationships with major powers, amid concerns about US reliability. The situation is part of a broader regional dynamic, with North Korea recently conducting tests of missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads. The Philippines' President Marcos is navigating a complex geopolitical landscape, seeking to protect his country's interests while maintaining relationships with both the US and China. The country's sovereignty and territorial claims remain a key priority.

stories.headline: Apple Seeds Second iOS 26.5 and iPadOS 26.5 Betas to Developers
stories.summary: Apple has seeded the second betas of upcoming iOS 26.5 and iPadOS 26.5 updates to developers for testing purposes. The software was released two weeks after Apple debuted the first betas. Registered developers can download the updated beta software using the Software Update section of the Settings app. The reason for the new beta updates is unclear, but it may be to address a bug fix that couldn't wait for the second beta to implement. The second iOS 26.5 beta is available for iPhone 11 and newer models, and it will soon be available to public beta testers. This release follows the first beta, which was re-released with minor updates two weeks ago. Developers can now test the new beta software to identify and report any issues before its public release.

stories.headline: Apple releases iOS 26.5 beta 2 for iPhone
stories.summary: Apple has released the second beta of iOS 26.5 for developers, which will soon be available to public beta testers. The beta update is compatible with iPhone 11 and newer models. This release follows the recent launch of a minor iOS 26.4.1 update for the same devices, which included unspecified bug fixes. Although the iOS 26.5 beta does not seem to deliver a significant Siri upgrade, it is expected to bring new features and improvements. Apple's developer pages highlight Apple Intelligence as a key feature, allowing developers to add personal intelligence to apps with great performance and built-in privacy. The release date for the final version of iOS 26.5 has not been officially announced, but it is expected to be part of Apple's next big update for iPhones.

stories.headline: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake Leak Confirms New Content
stories.summary: A leak of over an hour of footage from 007 First Light has revealed details about the Assassin's Creed Black Flag Remake, also known as Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced. The leak confirms that the game will receive new content, including new characters and stories, to modernize it for 2026. The remake aims to adapt the classic to contemporary narrative standards while remaining faithful to the original. The new content will fill gaps in the original story, breathing new life into the fan favorite. The game's description emphasizes its careful faithfulness to the spirit of the original, suggesting a balance between innovation and nostalgia. The leak has provided insight into Ubisoft's approach to remaking the classic game, indicating a significant update to the original Assassin's Creed Black Flag.

stories.headline: Teens Worry Over Growing Attachment to AI Chatbots
stories.summary: A recent study from Drexel University found that teenagers are concerned about developing unhealthy attachments to AI chatbots, such as Character.AI, which are becoming increasingly prevalent in their daily lives. These attachments are affecting their offline lives, prompting worries about their impact on adolescent mental health. Researchers, parents, and regulators are raising concerns about the potential effects of AI chatbots on young people. To address this issue, the research team advocates for a conscientious approach to AI chatbot design, introducing a framework that balances empathetic engagement with safeguards to discourage unhealthy attachments. The team suggests further study of larger communities of users to better understand the issue and mitigate potential harm. Designers are urged to build systems with empathy, nuance, and attention to detail to protect teens and promote resilience and growth.

stories.headline: US defender John Tolkin out injured for the 'coming weeks' ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: US defender John Tolkin will be out for several weeks with a knee ligament injury, his German club Holstein Kiel announced. The 23-year-old suffered a medial collateral ligament injury in a second-division game against Fortuna Duesseldorf on Friday. Tolkin is being treated conservatively, but his absence could impact the US men's national team ahead of the World Cup, which is less than two months away. This injury, combined with forward Patrick Agyemang's recent Achilles injury, may reduce the team's defensive depth. The US team's roster announcement is scheduled for May, and Tolkin's potential absence could affect the selection. The injury occurred just seven minutes into the match, and Tolkin's recovery time will be crucial in determining his availability for the World Cup.

stories.headline: Real Madrid’s objections have forced Barcelona to abandon their pursuit of a Spanish international
stories.summary: FC Barcelona's bid to sign Spanish international Victor Munoz has been blocked by Real Madrid. According to Spanish newspaper AS, Real Madrid are exercising their right of first refusal to prevent Munoz's move to their rivals. Munoz, a 22-year-old winger, previously played in Barcelona's youth academy from 2014 to 2017. Real Madrid's decision is reportedly due to their desire to prevent Munoz from joining their bitter rivals. AS reports that Real Madrid will "not allow" Munoz's possible arrival at Barcelona, citing the club's right of first refusal over the player's sporting future. As a result, Barcelona have been forced to abandon their pursuit of Munoz. The move is seen as a strategic decision by Real Madrid to limit Barcelona's strengthening of their squad.

stories.headline: Can Liverpool pull off yet another incredible Champions League comeback?
stories.summary: Liverpool faces a two-goal deficit against Paris Saint-Germain in their Champions League quarter-final tie. Captain Virgil van Dijk acknowledged that few people believe they can overturn the deficit. However, former player Alan Shearer is not ruling out a remarkable comeback. Arne Slot, whose team previously played Paris Saint-Germain, believes Anfield can inspire Liverpool to a famous comeback. The Athletic has analyzed how other teams have achieved positive results against the Ligue 1 side, providing potential strategies for Liverpool to turn the quarter-final around. Liverpool's chances seem slim, but the team has a history of impressive comebacks in the Champions League, making a turnaround not entirely impossible. The second leg will be crucial in determining the outcome of the tie.

stories.headline: The Republic of Ireland will head to Gdansk tomorrow without a point from their opening two games in this World Cup qualifying campaign.
stories.summary: The Republic of Ireland will travel to Gdansk without a point from their first two World Cup qualifying games. They lost to France and the Netherlands, placing them at the bottom of Group A2. Ahead of their match against Poland, manager Carla Ward announced that Ruesha will be unavailable for the Poland game but will return for the second match. The European teams will learn their 2026 World Cup qualifying opponents in a draw on Friday in Zurich. A total of 16 UEFA teams will qualify for the 48-team tournament, which will be held in the United States, Canada, and Mexico. The Republic of Ireland's next match is part of the World Cup 2026 qualifying campaign, with predictions and stats available. They face Poland in their next challenge, seeking to earn their first points in the campaign.

stories.headline: CM Revanth Proposes 'Fair Formula' for Delimitation
stories.summary: Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy has proposed a 'fair formula' for delimitation, advocating a hybrid model that considers both Gross State Domestic Product (GSDP) and population for fair seat distribution. He opposes the current delimitation proposal, which may nearly double the number of parliamentary and assembly seats in Telugu states. Reddy argues that seat expansion should reflect the contribution of southern states to India's economic growth. He plans to write to southern and smaller states to build a common front on the issue, emphasizing that delimitation is not just a party issue, but one that will shape the country's future balance of power. Former IAS officer EAS Sarma has termed Reddy's suggestion as not feasible, sparking a debate on the practicality of the proposed formula. National interest, Reddy says, must be placed above party interest.

stories.headline: India rain forecast: IMD forecasts below-average ‌monsoon rains for 2026 amid El Nino concerns
stories.summary: India's Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast a below-normal southwest monsoon for 2026, predicting rainfall at 92% of the long-period average (LPA) with a ±5% margin of error. The monsoon season, from June to September, is expected to receive below-normal rainfall due to shifting Pacific and Indian Ocean conditions and the potential development of El Niño. According to Ministry of Earth Sciences Secretary M. Ravichandran, monsoon rainfall will be "below normal" at 92% of the LPA. This forecast raises concerns over farm output and growth in the country. The IMD's long-range forecast was released on Monday, April 13, 2026, as reported by Zee News. The below-average monsoon rains may impact India's agricultural sector and overall economic growth. The forecast has a margin of error of ±5%, indicating a possible variation in the actual rainfall.

stories.headline: Workers’ protest over wage hike leads to unrest, violent face-off in Noida
stories.summary: Workers in Noida, Uttar Pradesh, protested over wage hike demands, which escalated into a violent face-off. The unrest was sparked by a significant wage hike in Gurgaon, where workers at another branch of the same company began earning substantially more. Protesters allegedly pelted stones, set a vehicle on fire, and vandalized properties, clashing with police and causing widespread disruption. The violence led to a citywide traffic collapse on Monday. Vehicles were torched, and stones were hurled, resulting in damage to properties. The protest turned violent, with reports of clashes between protesters and police, causing chaos in the city. The situation remains tense, with authorities working to restore order and address the workers' demands.

stories.headline: Who is Delhi HC's Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma, whom Arvind Kejriwal wants off his case
stories.summary: Delhi High Court Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma is at the center of a recusal plea by former Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal. Kejriwal has sought Justice Sharma's recusal from his case related to the Central Bureau of Investigation's (CBI) plea against his discharge in the alleged excise policy scam. Justice Sharma was elevated as a permanent judge of the Delhi High Court in March 2022 and has over three decades of judicial experience. She graduated in English Literature from Delhi University and acquired her law degrees in 1991 and 2004. Kejriwal's plea cites apprehension due to Justice Sharma's orders and conduct outside court. The Delhi High Court heard Kejriwal's case on Monday, with the former chief minister arguing his case personally. The recusal plea is pending before the court. Justice Sharma's impartiality has been questioned by Kejriwal in the matter.

stories.headline: Iran war and Strait of Hormuz: When geopolitics becomes grocery bills
stories.summary: The Strait of Hormuz, a crucial maritime choke point for global energy trade, has been disrupted since February 28, 2026, following joint US and Israel military strikes on Iran. The strait, located between Oman, the UAE, and Iran, connects the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea. Iran had blocked the waterway, a key oil shipping channel, in response to the attacks. However, a ceasefire has been agreed upon, conditional on guaranteeing "safe passage" through the strait. The blockade had affected global energy trade, with the strait being an international waterway previously open to all countries. The US and Israel's military campaign against Iran prompted the selective military blockade, which has now been eased with the ceasefire agreement, aiming to restore the strait's status as a secure international shipping route. The blockade's impact is being felt globally, including on grocery bills.

stories.headline: Hormuz reshapes global economic power dynamics, geopolitics and earnings will drive market direction, India’s climate tensions may bring economic cos
stories.summary: The 2026 Iran war has caused the largest supply disruption in global oil market history, according to the International Energy Agency. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has led to increased uncertainty, weighing on investment and trade. Rising geopolitical tensions are amplifying effects beyond the conflict, impacting the Indian economy through increased transit costs, energy prices, inflation, and fiscal stability. The US-Iran conflict has pushed the Strait to a de facto closure, putting 20% of global oil supply at risk. As a result, Brent prices are expected to rise, and gasoil and jet cracks are set to spike. Russia may benefit as India and China pivot away from disrupted Middle East supply, while shipping costs increase due to the fragile safe passage of oil carriers through the Strait. This has significant implications for global economic power dynamics and market direction.

stories.headline: Cyberattacks, Tariffs, Geopolitics Loom Over Business Executives
stories.summary: A report based on interviews with over 600 US executives found that 70% of business executives surveyed are concerned about cyberattacks, tariffs, and geopolitics. The executives worry most about cyber-enabled fraud, geopolitics, and AI. The report also found strong global support for cyber regulation, with 59% of risk leaders considering incorporating agentic AI solutions into their operations. However, 55% of those leaders admit to lacking preparedness for AI-related risks. The current trade landscape, marked by rising tariffs and shifting geopolitical alliances, has placed import-dependent business models under scrutiny. The Trump administration's approach to trade has made tariffs a primary instrument of economic power, forcing businesses to remain vigilant and proactive in navigating the complex environment. The report highlights the need for businesses to adapt to the changing landscape and prioritize risk management and regulation.

stories.headline: Why are US stock market futures down today, and will Dow Jones, S&P 500 and Nasdaq stay in red or turn green again?
stories.summary: US stock market futures are down due to failed talks between the US and Iran, which led to a surge in oil prices above $100. This has sparked inflation fears, causing investors to shift to safe assets. As a result, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.73% to 39,669.39, while the S&P 500 dropped 2.24% to 5,275.70, led by declines in the information technology sector. Travel stocks declined, but energy stocks gained. The market is now closely watching geopolitics and earnings season, which is just beginning. Investors are tracking inflation risks, Fed policy, and key stocks, amid concerns that the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq may remain in the red. Oil price increases and reduced hopes for rate cuts are also contributing to the market downturn.

stories.headline: The M-factor that may tip Bengal With women voters forming nearly half the electorate, the April 23 and 29 West Bengal polls have turned into a high-stakes battle over welfare, safety and representation
stories.summary: The West Bengal polls, scheduled for April 23 and 29, have become a high-stakes battle over welfare, safety, and representation, with women voters forming nearly half the electorate. The BJP and TMC are competing to win over this decisive voting bloc, with the TMC focusing on its established social coalition and the BJP using financial aid and identity politics. Early opinion polls indicate the TMC is currently leading, with the contest largely bipolar between the TMC and BJP. The Left parties and Congress are registering limited support, suggesting a marginal role. The elections will be decided in two phases, with counting on May 4. The outcome is being closely watched, as winning the women's vote in Bengal could set a playbook for the rest of the country. The parties' manifestos reveal strategic appeals to specific voter groups.

stories.headline: Biden Administration Unveils Plan to Address Border Crisis
stories.summary: The Biden administration is preparing to roll out a sweeping border executive action, potentially as early as Tuesday, according to sources familiar with the discussions. This move aims to address the ongoing crisis at the US-Mexico border. In September 2022, the administration unveiled a comprehensive immigration reform bill, which included a path to citizenship for millions of undocumented immigrants. The bill was designed to tackle the border crisis, amid criticism that the administration's immigration policy has led to an influx of migrants. A top official recently stated "the border is closed," pushing back against criticism of the administration's handling of immigration policy. The new executive action is expected to build upon previous efforts to reform immigration and secure the border. Timing for the rollout is subject to change.

stories.headline: US Senate Passes Landmark Bill to Combat Climate Change
stories.summary: The US Senate has passed a landmark bill to combat climate change, a significant measure to address the issue. The bill, known as the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), includes provisions on climate change, tax reform, energy policy, and health care. The passage of the bill was made possible by a recent deal between Democratic Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer and Senator Joe Manchin. The IRA is the first major legislation passed by the Senate to address climate change, marking a significant milestone. The bill's provisions aim to reduce carbon emissions and promote clean energy, with potential impacts on Americans and the economy. The Senate's approval of the bill is seen as a crucial step in the US's efforts to mitigate climate change, with the legislation now moving forward for further consideration.

stories.headline: European Union Leaders Reach Agreement on Immigration Reform
stories.summary: European Union leaders have reached an agreement on immigration reform, known as the New Pact on Migration and Asylum. The pact, set to take effect in June 2026, aims to evenly distribute the cost and efforts of hosting migrants among member states. It also reforms asylum and border security procedures, streamlines deportations, and increases detentions. The agreement was advanced by EU interior ministers in December 2025, with Italy's Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni playing a key role in building consensus on reducing irregular migration. The European Parliament has voted to expand the EU's detention and deportation plans, with the new rules compelling member states to share the financial burden of migration. The pact is a major overhaul of the EU's migration system, aiming to create a more unified and efficient approach to managing migration and asylum claims.

stories.headline: STG Announces Acquisition of Carrier Logistics Inc. to Pioneer AI-Agentic Platform for LTL and Last Mile Carriers
stories.summary: STG has acquired Carrier Logistics Inc. (CLI) to pioneer an AI-agentic platform for less-than-truckload (LTL) and last mile carriers. The acquisition will accelerate product innovation by integrating advanced AI frameworks into CLI's core architecture, driving towards an AI-native operating system. This system will automate and optimize terminal-based motor carrier operations. CLI customers can expect measurable operational improvements. The company's president, Ben Wiesen, stated that joining forces with STG will enhance support capabilities for customers while allowing the existing team to continue serving them. The investment aims to add agentic intelligence to automate execution, augmenting traditional LTL software that focuses on record-keeping and workflow orchestration. The acquisition is expected to significantly improve operations for CLI's customers, marking a significant step towards pioneering AI-agentic platforms in the industry.

stories.headline: Companies Rush to Market Only to See Deals Falter Over Financial Reporting Gaps
stories.summary: Commercial property deals in the Asia-Pacific region fell 37% in the third quarter compared to the same period in 2022, according to MSCI Real Assets. This marks a sixth consecutive quarter of year-on-year declines. The decline is attributed to financial reporting gaps, causing deals to falter. Companies rushing to market are facing challenges due to inadequate financial reporting, leading to a slowdown in transactions. The Asia-Pacific region has been experiencing a downturn in commercial property deals, with no signs of recovery in the near term. The decline is a significant concern for investors and businesses, highlighting the need for improved financial transparency and reporting standards in the region. MSCI Real Assets' data provides insight into the market trends, indicating a continued decline in commercial property deals.

stories.headline: Off-the-Shelf CAR T Shows 58.3% MRD Clearance in Pretreated LBCL Group
stories.summary: Allogene's off-the-shelf CAR-T therapy, cema-cel, demonstrated a 58.3% measurable residual disease (MRD) clearance rate in pretreated large B-cell lymphoma (LBCL) patients at day 45. The ALPHA3 trial, initiated in June 2024, evaluated cema-cel as part of a 1L consolidation treatment regimen for LBCL patients who achieved remission but tested positive for MRD. Compared to a 16.7% MRD-clearance rate in the observation arm, cema-cel showed significant improvement. Patients treated with cema-cel saw a median decrease of 97.7% in plasma ctDNA levels at day 45. The results suggest potential for cema-cel to change the approach to high-risk LBCL treatment at the end of first-line therapy, with early MRD clearance being an encouraging sign. The trial's findings support further investigation into cema-cel's efficacy in this setting.

stories.headline: Families stuck in Milan after EU border delay made them miss flight
stories.summary: 122 easyJet passengers were left stranded at Milan's Linate airport after missing their flight due to lengthy delays at EU border control. The new face-scanning and fingerprint border check system caused three-hour queues, resulting in around 100 people missing their flights. Travellers experienced overheated conditions, with some fainting and vomiting while waiting. A Leeds family was among those affected, despite arriving early for their flight. The EU's entry-exit system, which includes biometric checks, was the cause of the massive passport control delays. Only 34 passengers made it onto the easyJet flight, which departed without the majority of its scheduled passengers. The incident highlights the challenges posed by the new border control system, which has led to significant disruptions for travellers at Milan's Linate airport.
